name: Cause of the outcome of quantum events - Philosophy Stack Exchange
snippet: All quantum events have a cause, but the cause never determines the effect with absolute accuracy. That is because there is no such thing as absolute accuracy. Does quantum mechanics rule out the principle of sufficient reason? Are actually random events causeless?
